sentence_reviews.text: A generator conditions on retrieved non-parametric evidence so knowledge can be updated and attributed externally.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The RAG source directly describes generation conditioned on retrieved non-parametric memory/evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition matches the cited RAG mechanism.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Retrieval-augmented generation is to connect Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking to downstream capabilities including Graph RAG & structured retrieval, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking, Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The RAG source supports retrieval feeding generation and attribution to external knowledge; downstream Graph RAG/provenance links are plausible extensions.
sentence_reviews.notes: Multimodal assistants are not directly established by the cited RAG source.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.
